name: TXI
description:
TXI (Texture Extra Information) is a plain-text format used by KotOR I & II to store rendering hints for textures. The same format appears in two places: (1) as a standalone .txi file alongside a TGA texture (same base name, e.g. mytex.tga and mytex.txi), or (2) embedded at the end of a TPC file as a null-terminated string. In both cases the content is identical: a line-based list of directives (keyword plus optional value). Keywords are case-insensitive. Line endings are typically LF or CRLF. Encoding is usually ASCII or Windows-1252.

Structure: the file has no header or magic. Each line is one directive. Split the line on space to get the keyword (first token) and optional value tokens. Some directives consume multiple following lines (upperleftcoords N and lowerrightcoords N). Empty lines and unknown keywords are typically ignored. See the TXI Directives type for every supported property and its allowed values.

TXIFile.description: The entire file is plain text. Parse line by line. Each non-empty line is a directive: KEYWORD followed by optional value tokens. Keywords are case-insensitive. See TXI Directives for every property and its allowed values; see parsing steps for multi-line rules (upperleftcoords, lowerrightcoords).

fields.name: content
fields.description: Raw file content. Split on newlines; for each line, split on space; first token is the directive keyword, remainder is the value (or multi-line block for upperleftcoords/lowerrightcoords).

TXI Directives.description: All supported TXI directives. Each line in the file is one of these keywords followed by the value(s) described below. Order of lines is arbitrary; keywords are case-insensitive.

fields.name: isbumpmap
fields.description: Value: 0 or 1. 1 = texture is a bump map; 0 = not.

fields.name: islightmap
fields.description: No value. The keyword alone on a line marks the texture as a lightmap.

fields.name: cube
fields.description: No value. The keyword alone on a line marks the texture as an environment/cube map.

fields.name: compresstexture
fields.description: Value: 0 or 1. 1 = use compression; 0 = uncompressed.

fields.name: mipmap
fields.description: Value: integer. Mipmap setting (e.g. 0 = off, 1 = on).

fields.name: downsamplemin
fields.description: Value: integer. Downsample minimum.

fields.name: downsamplemax
fields.description: Value: integer. Downsample maximum.

fields.name: decal
fields.description: Value: integer. Decal mode (typically 0 or 1).

fields.name: defaultwidth
fields.description: Value: integer. Default width hint.

fields.name: defaultheight
fields.description: Value: integer. Default height hint.

fields.name: filter
fields.description: Value: integer. Texture filter mode.

fields.name: blending
fields.description: Value: literal string. Allowed values: punchthrough, additive.

fields.name: bumpmapscaling
fields.description: Value: float. Bump map scale factor.

fields.name: bumpmaptexture
fields.description: Value: texture resref or path (string). When embedded in TPC, strip null bytes and trailing garbage.

fields.name: bumpyshinytexture
fields.description: Value: texture resref or path (string). Same meaning as envmaptexture. Strip nulls when embedded.

fields.name: envmaptexture
fields.description: Value: texture resref or path (string). Environment map texture. Strip nulls when embedded.

fields.name: wateralpha
fields.description: Value: float. Water transparency/alpha.

fields.name: proceduretype
fields.description: Value: literal string. Allowed values: cycle (animated texture; use with numx, numy, fps), water, random, ringtexdistort.

fields.name: numx
fields.description: Value: integer. For proceduretype cycle: grid columns (frame count).

fields.name: numy
fields.description: Value: integer. For proceduretype cycle: grid rows (frame count).

fields.name: fps
fields.description: Value: integer. For proceduretype cycle: frames per second.

fields.name: numchars
fields.description: Value: integer. Number of characters (font textures).

fields.name: fontheight
fields.description: Value: float. Font height (font textures).

fields.name: baselineheight
fields.description: Value: float. Baseline height (font textures).

fields.name: texturewidth
fields.description: Value: float. Texture width hint (font textures).

fields.name: spacingr
fields.description: Value: float. Right spacing (font textures).

fields.name: spacingb
fields.description: Value: float. Bottom spacing (font textures).

fields.name: caretindent
fields.description: Value: float. Caret indent (font textures).

fields.name: upperleftcoords
fields.description: Value: integer N. Consumes the next N lines; each line is three floats x y z (upper-left corner of glyph rect).

fields.name: lowerrightcoords
fields.description: Value: integer N. Consumes the next N lines; each line is three floats x y z (lower-right corner of glyph rect).

sections.name: content
sections.description: Plain-text directive list. No binary header; file starts at 0. Parse as described in 'Parsing steps'. For each directive's allowed values, see type 'TXI Directives'.

layoutSteps.title: Parsing steps
steps: Read the entire file as text (encoding ASCII or Windows-1252). Normalize line endings to newline; split on newline to get lines.

For each line: trim optional leading/trailing whitespace. If the line is empty, skip it. Otherwise split the line on space (first token = keyword, remainder = value part). Keyword is case-insensitive.
Single-value directives: keyword is args[0], value is args[1] (parsed as int or float as needed). Strip null characters and trailing garbage from string values (e.g. bumpmaptexture, envmaptexture).

Multi-line directives: 'upperleftcoords N' — the value N is the count of following lines; each of the next N lines contains three floats (x y z), one per line. 'lowerrightcoords N' — same, N lines of x y z. Advance the line index past these N lines after consuming them.
Blending: only 'punchthrough' and 'additive' are defined. Proceduretype: 'cycle', 'water', 'random', 'ringtexdistort'. Unknown values may be treated as none.
Order: directives can appear in any order. Some engines use the first occurrence of a keyword; others may allow overrides.
